ucs.title: [LLM] Scattered Spider (Octo Tempest / UNC3944): RMM agent execution within hours of help-desk-driven password reset
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical Scattered Spider entry chain — vish/SMS social-engineering of an IT help-desk to reset a target user's password (T1078 / T1556), followed by interactive sign-in and rapid deployment of a portable Remote Monitoring & Management (RMM) tool (AnyDesk, Splashtop, ScreenConnect, TeamViewer, RustDesk, FleetDeck, Tactical RMM, Pulseway, Syncro, Atera, Ngrok) for hands-on-keyboard access (T1219). The combination of a recent admin-initiated password reset + a non-baselined RMM binary executing under that same UPN is a near-pathognomonic Octo Tempest signature.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as resetTime values(Authentication.src) as resetSrc from datamodel=Authentication where (Authentication.action="password_reset" OR Authentication.signature_id IN (4724,4723) OR Authentication.action="Reset user password") by Authentication.user Authentication.dest | rename Authentication.user as user Authentication.dest as resetDest | eval user=lower(user) | join type=inner user [| tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as procTime values(Processes.dest) as host values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("anydesk.exe","AnyDesk.exe","Splashtop.exe","SRService.exe","ScreenConnect.ClientService.exe","ScreenConnect.WindowsClient.exe","TeamViewer.exe","TeamViewer_Service.exe","rustdesk.exe","FleetDeck_Agent.exe","tacticalrmm.exe","PulsewayService.exe","Syncro.Service.exe","AteraAgent.exe","ngrok.exe","LevelAgent.exe","action1_agent.exe") AND Processes.user!="*$" by Processes.user | rename Processes.user as user | eval user=lower(replace(user,"^.*\\\\",""))] | eval delayMin=round((procTime-resetTime)/60,1) | where delayMin >= 0 AND delayMin <= 720 | table resetTime procTime delayMin user resetSrc resetDest host parent cmdline | sort - procTime
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowHours = 12h;
let RMMBins = dynamic(["anydesk.exe","splashtop.exe","sragent.exe","srservice.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","teamviewer.exe","teamviewer_service.exe","rustdesk.exe","fleetdeck_agent.exe","tacticalrmm.exe","pulsewayservice.exe","syncro.service.exe","ateraagent.exe","ngrok.exe","levelagent.exe","action1_agent.exe","msp360.exe"]);
let Resets = IdentityDirectoryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType in ("Account Password changed","Forced password reset","Password change attempt")
    | where isnotempty(TargetAccountUpn)
    | project ResetTime = Timestamp,
              ResetActor = AccountUpn,
              ResetIP = IPAddress,
              TargetUpn = tolower(TargetAccountUpn);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where FileName has_any (RMMBins)        // term-aligned, indexed
| where AccountName !endswith "$"          // skip machine accounts
| where isnotempty(AccountUpn)
| extend UserUpn = tolower(AccountUpn)
| join kind=inner Resets on $left.UserUpn == $right.TargetUpn
| where Timestamp between (ResetTime .. ResetTime + WindowHours)
| project ResetTime,
          ProcTime = Timestamp,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, ResetTime),
          DeviceName,
          TargetUpn,
          ResetActor,                       // help-desk admin who performed the reset
          ResetIP,
          RMMTool = FileName,
          ProcessCommandLine,
          ParentProcess = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowHours = 12h;
let RMMBins = dynamic(["anydesk.exe","splashtop.exe","sragent.exe","srservice.exe","screenconnect.clientservice.exe","screenconnect.windowsclient.exe","teamviewer.exe","teamviewer_service.exe","rustdesk.exe","fleetdeck_agent.exe","tacticalrmm.exe","pulsewayservice.exe","syncro.service.exe","ateraagent.exe","ngrok.exe","levelagent.exe","action1_agent.exe"]);
let Resets = AuditLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where OperationName in ("Reset user password","Reset password (by admin)","Admin reset user password","Change user password")
    | extend Initiator = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName),
             InitiatorIP = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress),
             TargetUpn = tolower(tostring(TargetResources[0].userPrincipalName))
    | where isnotempty(TargetUpn)
    | project ResetTime = TimeGenerated, Initiator, InitiatorIP, TargetUpn;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688                         // process-create (requires audit policy)
| where Account !endswith "$"
| where NewProcessName has_any (RMMBins)         // term-aligned
| extend UserSam = tolower(extract(@"\\([^\\]+)$", 1, Account))
| join kind=inner (
    Resets
    | extend UserSam = tolower(extract(@"^([^@]+)@", 1, TargetUpn))
  ) on UserSam
| where TimeGenerated between (ResetTime .. ResetTime + WindowHours)
| project ResetTime,
          ProcTime = TimeGenerated,
          DelayMin = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, ResetTime),
          Computer,
          TargetUpn,
          Initiator,                             // help-desk admin who reset the password
          InitiatorIP,
          RMMTool = NewProcessName,
          CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName
| order by ProcTime desc
ucs.rationale: Scattered Spider / Octo Tempest is uniquely characterised by tricking IT help-desks into resetting credentials (CISA AA23-320A, Microsoft DEV-0971/Storm-0875, Mandiant UNC3944) and then immediately deploying a portable RMM agent for hands-on access — they typically eschew custom malware and rely on legitimately-signed remote-access tooling. Joining the help-desk reset event to an RMM-binary execut

_field_issues.message: `IsInitiatingProcessRemoteSession` is not a canonical column of DeviceProcessEvents, IdentityDirectoryEvents. Did you mean `InitiatingProcessTokenElevation`?

ucs.title: [LLM] Scattered Spider (Octo Tempest): Rogue federated-domain / IdP federation modification in Entra ID
ucs.description: Detects Scattered Spider's signature long-term persistence move — adding or modifying a federated identity provider on an Entra ID tenant so the actor can mint SAML tokens for any user (T1556.006 / T1556.009 / T1484.002). Microsoft Threat Intel has repeatedly attributed this 'rogue federation' / Golden SAML-style trust manipulation to Octo Tempest after they obtain Global Admin via help-desk-reset MFA bypass.
ucs.splunk: `azure_audit` (operationName.value IN ("Set federation settings on domain","Set domain authentication","Add unverified domain","Add verified domain","Update domain","Update policy") OR properties.targetResources{}.modifiedProperties{}.displayName IN ("IssuerUri","PassiveSignInUri","SigningCertificate","FederationSettings")) | rename properties.initiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName as initiator properties.initiatedBy.user.ipAddress as src_ip properties.targetResources{}.displayName as targetDomain properties.result as result | eval src=coalesce(src_ip,"unknown") | stats min(_time) as firstSeen max(_time) as lastSeen values(operationName.value) as operations values(targetDomain) as domains values(src) as src_ips by initiator | where firstSeen > relative_time(now(),"-7d") | sort - firstSeen
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let FederationOps = dynamic([
    "Set federation settings on domain.",
    "Set domain authentication.",
    "Add unverified domain.",
    "Add verified domain.",
    "Update domain.",
    "Set company contact information."
]);
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where Application in~ ("Office 365","Microsoft Azure")
| where ActionType in~ (FederationOps)
| extend RawProps = tostring(RawEventData)
| extend ModifiedProps = extract_all(@"""DisplayName"":""([^""]+)""", RawProps)
| project Timestamp,
          ActionType,
          Actor = AccountDisplayName,
          ActorObjectId = AccountObjectId,
          IsAdmin = IsAdminOperation,
          IPAddress,
          CountryCode,
          City,
          ISP,
          IsAnonymousProxy,
          UserAgent,
          TargetDomain = ObjectName,
          ModifiedProps,
          RawProps
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let FederationOps = dynamic([
    "Set federation settings on domain",
    "Set domain authentication",
    "Add unverified domain",
    "Add verified domain",
    "Update domain"
]);
AuditLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where OperationName in~ (FederationOps)
   or (Category =~ "DirectoryManagement"
       and tostring(TargetResources) has_any ("IssuerUri","PassiveSignInUri","SigningCertificate","FederationSettings"))
| extend Initiator     = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.userPrincipalName),
         InitiatorAppId = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).app.appId),
         InitiatorIP   = tostring(parse_json(tostring(InitiatedBy)).user.ipAddress),
         TargetDomain  = tostring(TargetResources[0].displayName),
         ModifiedProps = TargetResources[0].modifiedProperties
| mv-expand ModifiedProps
| extend ChangedField = tostring(ModifiedProps.displayName),
         OldValue     = tostring(ModifiedProps.oldValue),
         NewValue     = tostring(ModifiedProps.newValue)
| where ChangedField in~ ("IssuerUri","PassiveSignInUri","SigningCertificate","NextSigningCertificate","FederationSettings","AuthenticationType","ActiveSignInUri")
   or OperationName in~ (FederationOps)
| project TimeGenerated,
          OperationName,
          Result,
          Initiator,
          InitiatorAppId,
          InitiatorIP,
          TargetDomain,
          ChangedField,
          OldValue,
          NewValue,
          CorrelationId
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Scattered Spider Rogue Federation / IdP Modification in Entra ID
id: 8a4f6f1c-5a9b-4c1f-9b1e-3c0e9a1b6d11
status: experimental
description: Detects addition or modification of a federated identity provider / domain in Entra ID - Octo Tempest (Scattered Spider) tradecraft for Golden-SAML-style persistence after help-desk-driven Global Admin compromise.
references:
  - https://www.microsoft.com/security/blog/2023/10/25/octo-tempest-crosses-boundaries-to-facilitate-extortion-encryption-and-destruction/
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/cybersecurity-advisories/aa23-320a
author: detection-engineering
date: 2026/05/04
tags:
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1556.006
  - attack.t1484.002
  - attack.g1015
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: auditlogs
detection:
  selection_op:
    properties.operationName:
      - 'Set federation settings on domain'
      - 'Set domain authentication'
      - 'Add unverified domain'
      - 'Add verified domain'
      - 'Update domain'
  selection_field:
    properties.targetResources.modifiedProperties.displayName:
      - 'IssuerUri'
      - 'PassiveSignInUri'
      - 'SigningCertificate'
      - 'NextSigningCertificate'
      - 'FederationSettings'
      - 'ActiveSignInUri'
  condition: selection_op or selection_field
fields:
  - properties.initiatedBy.user.userPrincipalName
  - properties.initiatedBy.user.ipAddress
  - properties.targetResources.displayName
  - properties.result
falsepositives:
  - Planned tenant migration to AD FS or third-party IdP (Okta, Ping, Auth0)
  - Certificate rollover by identity team (compare to change-control)
level: high
ucs.rationale: Microsoft Threat Intelligence (Octo Tempest blog, Oct 2023) and Mandiant UNC3944 reporting both call out Scattered Spider's habit of - once they obtain Global Admin via help-desk MFA bypass - adding a malicious federated domain or rotating an existing IdP's signing certificate / IssuerUri so they can sign SAML assertions for arbitrary identities. This is extremely rare in benign ops (a handful of
